% ppart-test.tex -- builds the two multi-part problems under \shuffle (pinned
% seed) so the atomicity/sub-numbering assertions in test_engine_correctness.py
% run against a shuffled question order.  The assertions are seed-invariant: they
% check each problem's own parts stay together and correctly lettered, whichever
% order the shuffle emits the problems in.
\documentclass{autoexam}
\versions{A}
\setexamseed{99}
\shuffle
\examsetup{number=1, date={Engine Test}}
\loadbank{ppart-bank.tex}

\begin{document}
\begin{problems}
	\problem{pone}
	\problem{ptwo}
\end{problems}
\end{document}
